info.title: Strata Public API
info.description:
Public API for Strata Mining Tools (celd.space). Provides ore discovery, location ranking, craftable item, and crafting-cost data derived from Star Citizen game extractions.

The API is key-gated (every endpoint requires a Bearer key). This reference and the spec downloads are public; get a key at https://strata.celd.space/api-keys. Keys are self-service and require Discord login, a verified email, and your real name on file.

Rate limit: 120 requests per 60 seconds per key. Please credit "data from celd.space" when surfacing this data publicly.

The underlying data only changes when Star Citizen ships a new patch (game-data is re-extracted then). Cache responses and refresh infrequently rather than polling; you will rarely approach the rate limit.

bearerAuth.description: A Strata API key in the form `celd_<key>`. Obtain one at https://strata.celd.space/api-keys.

id.description: Canonical ore identifier. Use this as the `{ore}` parameter in /ore-locations/{ore}. Stable across game patches.

name.description: Display name of the ore as shown in the Strata UI.

slug.description: Best-effort name slug (lowercase, no parenthetical suffixes). A handful of slugs collide across ores; prefer the canonical id when precision matters.

tier.description: Ore tier grouping from the game data. Null when not classified.

category.description: Ore category (e.g. "ship", "fps"). Differentiates surface/FPS ores from ship-mining ores.

scanSignature.description: Scan signature value used in the Strata signatures chart. Higher values are detected at longer range.

instability.description: Instability rating (0.0 to 1.0). 0 is valid data (not missing); use null-check, not truthiness. Higher values mean more dangerous extraction.

resistance.description: Rock resistance rating (0.0 to 1.0). Higher means harder to break.

ores.description: All minable ores, sorted alphabetically by name.

count.description: Total number of ores in the array.

attribution.description: Attribution string. Please include in any public display.
attribution.example: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

id.description: Canonical location identifier. Use this as the `{location}` parameter in /location-ores/{location}. Stable across game patches for named locations.

slug.description: Best-effort name slug. Prefer the canonical id for unambiguous lookups.

system.description: Star system. Open string (new systems are added over time). Examples: Stanton, Pyro, Nyx.

type.description: Location type from the game data (e.g. belt, moon, dungeon, planet, lagrange_point).

locations.description: All mineable locations (those with at least one ore spawn), sorted alphabetically by name. Aggregate placeholder rows are excluded.

count.description: Total number of locations in the array.

attribution.description: Attribution string. Please include in any public display.
attribution.example: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

locationId.description: Canonical location identifier. Stable across game patches for named locations.

system.description: Star system. Open string (new systems are added over time). Examples: Stanton, Pyro, Nyx.

rankPct.description: 0-100. How much of this ore is at this location relative to other locations in the same group. 100 = the top-ranked location. Only comparable within a group.

rank.description: Competition rank within the group (1 = best). Ties receive the same rank.

n.description: Total number of locations in this group that have any spawn of this ore. Useful for normalizing rank.

concentration.description: This ore's share of the total mining presence at this location (0.0 to 1.0, 4 decimal places). Measures what fraction of rocks here contain this ore. This is an absolute value, not a percentile or cross-location rank -- a concentration of 0.30 at an asteroid field and a concentration of 0.30 on a moon surface are directly comparable.

tier.description: Concentration tier based on absolute thresholds applied to the concentration value (0 = None, 1 = Negligible, 2 = Trace, 3 = Minor, 4 = Notable, 5 = Major, 6 = Pure). These tiers are absolute bands, not percentiles -- the same thresholds apply across all location types. Tiers are directly comparable across space, surface, cave, and exploration groups.

tierName.description: Human-readable tier label corresponding to the tier integer. Fixed enum: None (0), Negligible (1), Trace (2), Minor (3), Notable (4), Major (5), Pure (6). Tier 0 None appears in tierLegend but is never returned in a location row.

id.description: Canonical ore id from game data. Use this as your key for subsequent requests.

sort.description: The sort order applied to the results arrays. Echoes the request query param.

tierLegend.description: Maps tier integer (as string key) to tier name. Keys 0-6; tier values in results range 1-6 (tier 0 None exists in the legend but is never returned in a location row).

results.description: Location rows grouped by environment type. Any group may be an empty array if this ore has no spawns there. Do not compare rankPct across groups.

space.description: Asteroid fields, belts, asteroid clusters, Lagrange stations, and other non-surface non-dungeon locations.

surface.description: Planet and moon surfaces (surface spawns, ROC mining).

exploration.description: Large exploration regions (Aaron Halo, Glaciem Ring, Keeger Belt). These use a density-scale ranking formula and are ranked against each other only, not against space or surface locations.

attribution.description: Attribution string. Please include in any public display.
attribution.example: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

oreId.description: Canonical ore identifier. Use this as the {ore} parameter in /ore-locations/{ore}.

concentration.description: This ore's share of the total mining presence at this location (0.0 to 1.0, 4 decimal places). Measures what fraction of rocks here contain this ore. This is an absolute value -- directly comparable across location types. A concentration of 0.30 at an asteroid field means the same thing as 0.30 on a moon surface.

tier.description: Concentration tier based on absolute thresholds applied to the concentration value. These tiers are absolute bands (not percentiles) and are directly comparable across location types: None (0), Negligible (1), Trace (2), Minor (3), Notable (4), Major (5), Pure (6).

tierName.description: Human-readable tier label. Fixed enum values: None (0), Negligible (1), Trace (2), Minor (3), Notable (4), Major (5), Pure (6).

scanSignature.description: Scan signature value from the game data. Useful for identifying this ore on the scanner.

system.description: Star system the location belongs to.

type.description: Location type from the game data.

ores.description: All ores present at this location, sorted by concentration descending.

count.description: Total number of ores in the array.

attribution.description: Attribution string. Please include in any public display.
attribution.example: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

id.description: Canonical item identifier (entity GUID from game data). Use this as the {item} parameter in /craft-cost/{item}. More reliable than slug -- slug collisions are expected across hundreds of items.

slug.description: Best-effort name slug (lowercase, hyphens). Collisions are expected across this catalog; use id for unambiguous lookups.

category.description: Item category label (e.g. Ship Components, FPS Weapons, FPS Armor).

items.description: All craftable items, sorted alphabetically by name.

count.description: Total number of craftable items in the array.

attribution.description: Attribution string. Please include in any public display.
attribution.example: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

material.description: Display name of the required ore or material.

oreId.description: Canonical ore id. Use this to look up additional ore data via /ores or /ore-locations/{ore}.

quantity.description: Amount required. Unit depends on the ore category.

unit.description: Unit for the quantity field. SCU for ship-mining ores. gems for FPS and ground-vehicle ores (item count, not SCU -- 1000 gems = 1 SCU). Never compare gem quantities to SCU quantities without converting.

minQuality.description: Minimum ore quality required (0-1000 scale). Null when no minimum is specified.

craftTime.description: Craft time in seconds. Null when not specified in the game data.

costs.description: List of materials required to craft this item.

attribution.description: Attribution string. Please include in any public display.
attribution.example: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

hint.example: Use an ore id (e.g. "beryl_raw") or a name slug (e.g. "beryl").

get.description:
Returns all minable ores with full metadata, sorted alphabetically by name. Use the returned `id` values as the `{ore}` parameter in `/api/public/ore-locations/{ore}`.

This is the discovery endpoint. Call it once (or once per patch cycle) and cache the result. The ore list only changes when Star Citizen ships a new game patch.

example.attribution: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

value.error: Missing API key. Send: Authorization: Bearer <key>

invalid.summary: Key revoked or does not exist

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check the `Retry-After` response header (value in seconds) before retrying.

get.description:
Returns ore abundance and ranking data for a single ore, grouped by location type (space, surface, cave, exploration). Results within each group are sorted by the requested `sort` parameter.

The `{ore}` parameter accepts either a canonical ore id (e.g. `beryl_raw`) or a name slug (e.g. `beryl`). Prefer canonical ids; name slugs are best-effort and a handful of ore names slug-collide.

parameters.name: ore
parameters.description: Ore identifier. Either a canonical ore id (e.g. `beryl_raw`, `iron_ore`) or a name slug (e.g. `beryl`, `iron`). Canonical ids are exact-match and always unambiguous. Name slugs strip `(Raw)` / `(Ore)` suffixes and lowercase before matching.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order for location rows within each group. `rank` sorts by `rankPct` descending (highest abundance first). `concentration` sorts by the fraction of rocks at each location that contain this ore.

example.attribution: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

value.error: Missing API key. Send: Authorization: Bearer <key>

invalid.summary: Key revoked or does not exist

404.description: Ore not found. The `{ore}` parameter did not match any known ore id or name slug.

example.hint: Use an ore id (e.g. "beryl_raw") or a name slug (e.g. "beryl").

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check the `Retry-After` response header (value in seconds) before retrying.

get.description:
Returns all locations that have at least one ore spawn, sorted alphabetically by name. Aggregate placeholder rows are excluded. Use the returned `id` values as the `{location}` parameter in `/api/public/location-ores/{location}`.

This is the discovery endpoint for the location family. Cache it; it only changes on new game patches.

example.attribution: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

value.error: Missing API key. Send: Authorization: Bearer <key>

invalid.summary: Key revoked or does not exist

get.description:
Returns all ores present at a given location with concentration and tier data, sorted by concentration descending.

Important: `concentration` is the ore's share of the total mining presence at this location (0.0 to 1.0). The `tier` and `tierName` values are absolute bands on that share, not a percentile or cross-location rank. A concentration of 0.30 and tier 6 (Pure) at an asteroid field means exactly the same thing as 0.30 and tier 6 on a moon surface. Values are directly comparable across all location types.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: Location identifier. Either a canonical location id (e.g. `ita_01`) or a name slug (e.g. `ita-belt-alpha`). Canonical ids are always unambiguous. Enumerate valid ids via `GET /api/public/locations`.

example.attribution: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

value.error: Missing API key. Send: Authorization: Bearer <key>

invalid.summary: Key revoked or does not exist

example.hint: Use a location id or name slug from GET /api/public/locations.

get.description:
Returns all craftable items, sorted alphabetically by name. Use the returned `id` (entity GUID) as the `{item}` parameter in `/api/public/craft-cost/{item}`.

This endpoint returns only items that have a crafting recipe with at least one cost. It does not include shop-only items. Cache this response; it only changes on new game patches.

example.attribution: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

value.error: Missing API key. Send: Authorization: Bearer <key>

invalid.summary: Key revoked or does not exist

get.summary: Get crafting cost for an item
get.description:
Returns the material cost to craft a single item. The `{item}` parameter is the entity GUID from `/api/public/items`.

Note the `unit` field on each cost: ship-mining ores use `SCU`; FPS and ground-vehicle ores use `gems` (item count, not SCU -- 1000 gems = 1 SCU). Never compare gem quantities to SCU quantities without converting.

parameters.name: item
parameters.description: Item entity GUID from GET /api/public/items. Also accepts a name slug as a fallback, but slug collisions are common across hundreds of items; prefer the id.

example.attribution: Data from celd.space / Strata Mining Tools

value.error: Missing API key. Send: Authorization: Bearer <key>

invalid.summary: Key revoked or does not exist

404.description: Item not found or not craftable.

example.hint: Use an item id from GET /api/public/items.
